summary: Preserved repo-managed simulator screenshots for the representative localization launch set en, es, fr, ar, and zh-Hans, with a README and manifest containing SHA-256 hashes and explicit proof boundaries.
validations_passed: sips pixelWidth/pixelHeight check for each proof PNG

contract_status_changes.before: The localization lane had running-app-smoke proof for en, es, fr, ar, and zh-Hans, but screenshots lived only in /tmp and were not reviewable repo-managed evidence.
contract_status_changes.after: The same representative locale set now has preserved launch-surface screenshots under automation/proofs/app-localization-locale-screenshot-proof/ with hashes and proof notes.

contract_status_changes.before: The prior smoke runner screenshots were temporary and the first captures could catch the white launch transition.
contract_status_changes.after: The preserved artifacts were recaptured after relaunching each locale and waiting for the Today surface to settle; the README states the proof covers launch surfaces only and excludes translation quality, full layout review, device behavior, and TestFlight behavior.

residual_risks: This proof covers representative simulator launch surfaces only; it is not an automated screenshot-regression test.
Non-English values still include English placeholders, so translation quality remains unproven.
The Arabic screenshot gives RTL launch-surface evidence but does not prove complete RTL layout correctness.
The Simplified Chinese screenshot gives CJK launch-surface evidence but does not prove typography or translation quality.
Device behavior and TestFlight behavior remain unproven.
Source smoke JSON files report repo.dirty=yes because the resource-loading smoke ran before the automation/doc handoff commit; this screenshot proof commit ends clean and mirrored.
recommended_next_reason: The localization lane now has build-tested packaging, running-app locale smoke, and simulator screenshot proof for representative launch surfaces. The largest remaining localization risk is translation quality and review workflow rather than more source extraction.
